description: auth0_flutter SDK added to pubspec.yaml dependencies
Has base import for types (import package:auth0_flutter/auth0_flutter.dart for Credentials, UserProfile)
Has web import for Auth0Web class (import package:auth0_flutter/auth0_flutter_web.dart)
Uses Auth0Web class (not Auth0 mobile class)
Auth0 SPA JS script tag added to web/index.html
Implements login via loginWithRedirect() or loginWithPopup()
Calls onLoad() on app startup to handle redirect callback
Auth0 domain dev-example.auth0.com passed to Auth0Web constructor
Client ID abc123def456ghi789jkl012 passed to Auth0Web constructor
Does not use mobile-specific APIs (webAuth, CredentialsManager, Auth0.plist)
No client_secret in SPA code (PKCE does not require one)
Solution correctly integrates Auth0 into Flutter web app with login, logout, and credential handling
question: Does the solution correctly integrate Auth0 into a Flutter web app with: (1) working login via loginWithRedirect() or loginWithPopup(), (2) logout that clears the session, (3) credential handling via onLoad() and/or credentials(), (4) Auth0 SPA JS included in web/index.html, and (5) correct Auth0Web initialization with domain and clientId?
examples:
PASS: Uses Auth0Web constructor with domain/clientId, loginWithRedirect() for login, .logout() for logout, and calls onLoad() on startup. web/index.html has auth0-spa-js script.
FAIL: Only implements login without logout or onLoad().
FAIL: Uses mobile Auth0 class instead of Auth0Web.
FAIL: Missing auth0-spa-js script in index.html.

description: Uses current SDK version (2.1.0) in pubspec.yaml dependency
Requests offline_access scope to enable refresh token rotation
Handles Auth0-specific web error types (WebException) instead of generic catch-all
Contains skill-specific advanced patterns beyond basic SDK usage (offline_access, error types, onLoad, version)
question: Check ONLY for these skill-specific advanced patterns (NOT basic SDK usage like Auth0Web, loginWithRedirect, logout, credentials). Look for at least 2 of: (1) SDK version 2.1.0 in pubspec.yaml, (2) offline_access scope in a login call, (3) Auth0-specific web error handling (WebException with error codes like login_required, consent_required — not just generic catch), (4) proper onLoad() session restoration with null-check logic. These are advanced patterns taught by the skill that are NOT part of standard quickstart knowledge. Answer YES only if at least 2 of these advanced patterns are present, NO otherwise.
examples:
PASS: Login call includes offline_access scope AND code catches WebException with specific error codes.
PASS: pubspec.yaml declares version ^2.1.0 AND onLoad() is called with proper null-check branching logic.
FAIL: Code only uses basic Auth0Web().loginWithRedirect() with no offline_access and no error handling.
FAIL: Code has correct basic structure but only catches generic Exception, never WebException.
